finding: May be associated with: Fitz-Hugh-Curtis syndrome (see below

differentials.name: usually bilateral dull aching lower abdominal pain 7-10 days after menstruation ← peritoneal irritation
differentials.raw: usually bilateral dull aching lower abdominal pain 7-10 days after menstruation ← peritoneal irritation

differentials.name: "chandelier" sign = patient reaching for the chandelier ← intense pain during pelvic examination
differentials.raw: "chandelier" sign = patient reaching for the chandelier ← intense pain during pelvic examination

differentials.name: fever, leukocytosis, ↑ ESR; ↑ serum C-reactive protein
differentials.raw: fever, leukocytosis, ↑ ESR; ↑ serum C-reactive protein

differentials.name: Infertility: 8%
differentials.raw: Infertility: 8% [20%] {40%} after single [2] {≥ 3} episodes of PID ← tubal occlusion (in 25%)

differentials.name: fever
differentials.raw: fever (most common cause of postpartum fever)

differentials.name: right upper quadrant pain ← ascending pelvic infection
differentials.raw: right upper quadrant pain ← ascending pelvic infection

differentials.name: often beginning during / immediately after menstruation ← mucus barrier of cervix less effective
differentials.raw: often beginning during / immediately after menstruation ← mucus barrier of cervix less effective

differentials.name: nodular thickening of isthmic portion of tube
differentials.raw: nodular thickening of isthmic portion of tube

differentials.name: Adhesions from prior episodes of PID
differentials.raw: Adhesions from prior episodes of PID

differentials.name: asymptomatic / recurrent lower abdominal pain, infertility
differentials.raw: asymptomatic / recurrent lower abdominal pain, infertility

differentials.name: secondary involvement of adjacent structures
differentials.raw: secondary involvement of adjacent structures (eg, ileus, hydroureteronephrosis) DDx:  endometrioma, ovarian tumor, infected cyst, abscess from other sources (eg, Crohn disease, appendicitis)

context: US: √  No findings √  thickened tubular adnexal structures ± central fluid √  increased adnexal volume √  periadnexal inflammatory hyperemia √  loss of normal tissue planes + ill-defined uterus: √  echogenic periovarian fat √  ± free fluid CT: √  nonspecific stranding of parapelvic fat √  thickening of uterosacral ligaments MR: √  ill-defined hyperintense parametrial area on fat-suppressed T2WI + intense enhancement on contrast-enhanced fat-suppressed MR (= extent of inflammation) Dx:   clinically, laparoscopically ◊  Imaging employed only to differentiate between medical + surgical condition
